questions.question: Explain the purpose of the Current Program Status Register (CPSR) in ARM processors.
keyPoints: CPSR is a 32-bit register that stores processor state information
It contains condition flags (N, Z, C, V) indicating results of operations
It contains control bits (I, F, T) for interrupt and instruction set control
It contains mode bits (M) that define the processor operating mode
It monitors execution state and controls processor operation

questions.question: Describe the four condition flags in CPSR and their significance.
keyPoints: N (Negative) flag: Set when result MSB is 1, indicates negative result
Z (Zero) flag: Set when result is zero, used for comparisons
C (Carry) flag: Set on unsigned overflow/borrow
V (Overflow) flag: Set on signed arithmetic overflow
These flags determine conditional branch outcomes

questions.question: What is the difference between CPSR and SPSR?
keyPoints: CPSR is Current Program Status Register - always holds current processor state
SPSR is Saved Program Status Register - stores CPSR during exceptions
Each exception mode has its own SPSR
SPSR allows restoration of processor state after exception handling
User mode does not have an SPSR

questions.question: Explain the processor modes supported by ARM and how they are selected in CPSR.
keyPoints: 7 processor modes: User, FIQ, IRQ, Supervisor, Abort, Undefined, System
Mode bits M[4:0] in CPSR select the operating mode
User and System are privileged modes
FIQ and IRQ are interrupt modes
Supervisor, Abort, Undefined are exception modes

questions.question: What happens to the CPSR when an exception is triggered in ARM?
keyPoints: Current CPSR is automatically saved to SPSR of the target exception mode
PC is saved to Link Register (LR) of the new mode
Processor switches to the appropriate exception mode
I and F bits are set to disable interrupts
Processor jumps to exception vector address

questions.question: Explain the function of I, F, and T bits in CPSR.
keyPoints: I bit (bit 7): When set to 1, disables IRQ (standard interrupts)
F bit (bit 6): When set to 1, disables FIQ (fast interrupts)
T bit (bit 5): T=0 indicates ARM state (32-bit instructions), T=1 indicates Thumb state (16-bit instructions)
These are control bits that modify processor operation

questions.question: Describe the Q flag in CPSR and its usage.
keyPoints: Q flag is the saturation flag in CPSR
Used in DSP (Digital Signal Processing) instructions
Set to 1 when saturation occurs during saturating arithmetic
Saturation limits values to maximum/minimum instead of wrapping around
Helps prevent audio processing overflow distortions

questions.question: With a neat diagram, explain the bit fields of CPSR. Discuss the condition flags and control bits in detail.
keyPoints: Draw 32-bit CPSR showing all bit fields
Bits 31-28: N, Z, C, V condition flags (explain each)
Bit 27: Q saturation flag
Bit 24: J flag for Jazelle state
Bit 5: T flag for ARM/Thumb state
Bits 7-6: I and F interrupt disable bits
Bits 4-0: Mode bits M[4:0]
Explain privilege levels and accessible bits in User mode vs privileged modes

questions.question: Explain the exception handling mechanism in ARM with respect to CPSR and SPSR. Also discuss how the processor returns from exception.
keyPoints: Types of exceptions: Reset, Data Abort, FIQ, IRQ, Prefetch Abort, SWI, Undefined instruction
On exception: CPSR saved to SPSR_mode, PC saved to LR_mode, Mode changes, I/F bits set
Return from exception using SUBS PC, LR, #4 or similar instructions
Restore CPSR from SPSR using MSR instruction
Explain difference in return addresses for different exceptions

questions.question: Write an ARM assembly program to demonstrate the use of CPSR flags. Include instructions that set and test these flags.
keyPoints: Use MOV, ADD, SUB instructions to show flag modification
Use CMP to compare values and observe flag changes
Use conditional execution (EQ, NE, GT, LT, etc.) based on flags
Show how flags affect branch instructions
Demonstrate with actual register values and expected flag states
Include explanation of how each instruction affects CPSR
